response.justification: The candidate has roughly 19 years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, satisfying the raw experience minimum. However, the career trajectory is consistently senior/staff-level individual product work rather than the director-level scope this role demands: there is no evidence of setting technical strategy across organizations, advising strategic customers, or leading disruptive initiatives at scale. AI exposure is recent and thin (MCP code generators and agent tooling since 2025), falling well short of 'recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms.' The founder experience with an open-source geo-data platform is a genuine entrepreneurial signal, but overall the profile fits a strong senior/staff engineer role, not a principal AI-strategy position.
strengths: Nearly two decades of broad, hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of a long-running open-source geo-data platform used in production
Strong craftsmanship and knowledge-sharing profile: TDD, open-source libraries, conference talks, and multi-day workshops
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth beyond recent agent-tooling contributions; no shipped AI products or platforms
No evidence of director-level scope: org-wide technical strategy, customer advisory, or leading large cross-team initiatives
No advanced degree in an AI-related field, and cloud experience appears limited to application-level usage (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and shipped AI products
Seniority gap between senior IC work and principal/strategy scope
Long, broad engineering experience plus founder/startup background
